cervical_pails_rails_flexion_l1.name: Cervical Flexion PAILs/RAILs - L1 (Teaching / Sub-max)
cervical_pails_rails_flexion_l1.pail_instruction: Contract the stretched tissue - push INTO the end-range position at target effort level
cervical_pails_rails_flexion_l1.rail_instruction: Contract the antagonist - pull deeper INTO the stretch, actively driving end range
cues: Find the stretch first, don't rush
Ramp up slow - 3 seconds to max effort at 30%
Focus on breathing, not grinding

Focus on breathing, not grinding

shoulder_pails_rails_anterior_capsule_l1.pail_instruction: Push the arm DOWN and FORWARD into the floor/strap as if resisting external rotation

cues: Find the stretch first, don't rush
Ramp up slow - 3 seconds to max effort at 30%
Focus on breathing, not grinding

shoulder_prh_flexion_wall.name: Shoulder Flexion PRH (Wall Slide End Position)
shoulder_prh_flexion_wall.setup: Back against wall, arms overhead in a goalpost position reaching toward the wall. Elbows and wrists pressed against the wall at full available shoulder flexion. Ribs down, glutes on - no lumbar arch to cheat depth.
shoulder_prh_flexion_wall.execution: Hold the end-range overhead position with elbows and wrists maintaining wall contact. Active but passive - not pushing into the wall, just maintaining the shape.
indications: Need to build tolerance at end-range before active work
Introduction to end-range training for new clients
contraindications: Acute shoulder pain at overhead position
Cannot reach wall with both elbows without lumbar extension
shoulder_prh_flexion_wall.integration_notes: Gateway to overhead pressing progressions. If client can't tolerate 30s PRH, overhead pressing is premature. Often paired with T-spine extension work - shoulder flexion restriction frequently comes from T-spine.

shoulder_prh_er_90_abducted.name: Shoulder ER PRH (90/90 Abducted)
shoulder_prh_er_90_abducted.setup: Supine (preferred entry) or standing with back against wall. Shoulder abducted to 90 degrees, elbow bent to 90 degrees, forearm rotated back into external rotation. In supine, forearm rests on floor/block at full available ER.
shoulder_prh_er_90_abducted.execution: Hold the ER position passively. The forearm should be at end-range of ER without shoulder hiking toward ear.
indications: Overhead athletes, throwers, clients doing pressing work
shoulder_prh_er_90_abducted.progression_from: Shoulder CARs cleared, ER tolerated through range
shoulder_prh_er_90_abducted.integration_notes: Critical for clients doing any pressing work. ER at 90 abduction is the position that prevents anterior shoulder irritation under load. Often the missing link between PAILs/RAILs work and successful bench/overhead pressing.

shoulder_prh_ir_quadruped.name: Shoulder IR PRH (Quadruped Reach-Through)
shoulder_prh_ir_quadruped.setup: Quadruped position. Thread one arm under the body, palm up, resting on floor. The supporting shoulder is loaded in IR + horizontal adduction. Opposite hand stays grounded.
shoulder_prh_ir_quadruped.execution: Hold the reach-through position. Feel the stretch in the posterior shoulder capsule and rhomboid area.
indications: Clients with anterior shoulder tightness from pressing work
shoulder_prh_ir_quadruped.integration_notes: Posterior capsule work is the most under-programmed end-range in adult training. Pairs well with IR PAILs/RAILs in the same block. Most shoulder pressing issues resolve when posterior capsule opens up.

shoulder_prh_extension_seated.name: Shoulder Extension PRH (Seated Reach-Back)
shoulder_prh_extension_seated.setup: Seated with hands placed on the floor behind the hips, fingers pointing away from body. Shoulders are in extension + slight IR. Chest up, don't let the shoulders hike.
shoulder_prh_extension_seated.execution: Walk hands slightly further back until full shoulder extension is reached. Hold. Breathe.
contraindications: Recent biceps tendon issues (biceps stretches in this position)
shoulder_prh_extension_seated.progression_from: Shoulder CARs cleared in extension range
shoulder_prh_extension_seated.integration_notes: Often overlooked. Shoulder extension loads biceps tendon and anterior capsule - valuable for adults with forward-head/rounded-shoulder postures. Pair with T-spine extension work.

shoulder_prlo_flexion_wall.name: Shoulder Flexion PRLO (Wall Lift-Off)
shoulder_prlo_flexion_wall.setup: Same as shoulder_prh_flexion_wall - back against wall, arms overhead at end range.
shoulder_prlo_flexion_wall.execution: From the PRH position, briefly lift one or both arms off the wall 1-2 inches, hold for 2-3 seconds, return to wall. This is the transition from passive tolerance to active control.
shoulder_prlo_flexion_wall.dose: 3 x 5-8 lift-offs per side. 2-3 second hold per rep. 45-60s rest.
indications: Owns shoulder flexion PRH at 30+ seconds
Ready to convert passive to active range
contraindications: Cannot maintain PRH without lumbar compensation
shoulder_prlo_flexion_wall.progression_to: shoulder_hover_flexion OR loaded shoulder flexion CARs
shoulder_prlo_flexion_wall.integration_notes: This is THE gap-closer for active overhead range. Client who can reach overhead passively but not actively lives here for 2-4 weeks. Pairs with the landmine press progression.

shoulder_prlo_er_abducted.name: Shoulder ER PRLO (90/90 Lift-Off)
shoulder_prlo_er_abducted.setup: Supine, shoulder abducted 90, elbow at 90, forearm resting at end range of ER on floor or block.
shoulder_prlo_er_abducted.execution: From the PRH position, lift the back of the hand off the floor/block 1-2 inches using active ER. Hold 2-3 seconds, return. No shoulder hike. No lumbar arch.
shoulder_prlo_er_abducted.dose: 3 x 5-8 lift-offs per side. 2-3 second hold. 45-60s rest.
indications: Rotator cuff activation at end range
Building ER strength for pressing health
shoulder_prlo_er_abducted.progression_to: shoulder_err_er OR loaded cuban press variations
shoulder_prlo_er_abducted.integration_notes: Direct prep for any overhead pressing. Most rotator cuff weakness lives in the top few degrees of ER - exactly what this exercise trains. Program 2-4 weeks before heavy press blocks.

shoulder_prlo_ir_quadruped.name: Shoulder IR PRLO (Reach-Through Lift)
shoulder_prlo_ir_quadruped.setup: Same as shoulder_prh_ir_quadruped - threaded arm under body in quadruped.
shoulder_prlo_ir_quadruped.execution: From the threaded position, briefly lift the threaded hand/forearm off the floor 1-2 inches. Hold 2-3 seconds. Return. Don't rotate the trunk to cheat.
shoulder_prlo_ir_quadruped.dose: 3 x 5-8 per side. 2-3 second hold. 30-45s rest.
contraindications: Inability to maintain quadruped without trunk compensation
shoulder_prlo_ir_quadruped.integration_notes: Builds active IR at end range - under-trained capacity in most adults. Pairs with any shoulder or pulling work.

shoulder_err_er.name: Shoulder ERR (External Rotation End Range Rotation)
shoulder_err_er.setup: Supine (easier) or side-lying. Shoulder at 90 degrees abduction, elbow 90. Start at end range of ER.
shoulder_err_er.execution: Slow active rotation between the deepest ER position and approximately 10-15 degrees short of that. NOT full ROM - this stays in the end-range zone only. Slow (5-8 seconds per direction).
shoulder_err_er.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow rotations per side. 45-60s rest.
indications: Owns PRLO cleanly at end range
Building active control across the end-range zone
contraindications: Pain during rotation at end range
shoulder_err_er.integration_notes: ERR is the 'learning to move at end range' phase. Neurologically different from CARs - slower, smaller range, end-focused. Builds active range that transfers to pressing and reaching overhead.

shoulder_err_ir.name: Shoulder ERR (Internal Rotation End Range Rotation)
shoulder_err_ir.setup: Quadruped with threaded arm under body, OR side-lying with top arm across body. Shoulder in deep IR.
shoulder_err_ir.execution: Slow active rotation between deepest IR and 10-15 degrees short, without backing out of IR range.
indications: Athletes needing IR for sport demands
shoulder_err_ir.integration_notes: Rare in general training but critical for overhead athletes and clients with IR deficits driving anterior shoulder problems.

shoulder_hover_flexion.name: Shoulder Flexion Hover (Overhead Sustained Lift)
shoulder_hover_flexion.setup: Back against wall, arms overhead in flexion end range (same start as PRH).
shoulder_hover_flexion.execution: Lift both arms off the wall 1-3 inches and HOLD for 15-30 seconds. Active end-range capacity - the hover is the transition from brief lift-off to sustained active end-range hold.
indications: Owns PRLO cleanly at 6+ reps with 2-3s holds
Pre-requisite for any heavy overhead loading
contraindications: Cannot hold lift without compensation for 5+ seconds
shoulder_hover_flexion.progression_to: Loaded overhead work OR shoulder CARs under load
shoulder_hover_flexion.integration_notes: Hovers are the ultimate end-range capacity test. A client who can hover shoulder flexion for 30 seconds has real active overhead range. Most adults will never own this - and don't need to for non-athletic demands.

shoulder_hover_er.name: Shoulder ER Hover (Sustained Lift-Off)
shoulder_hover_er.setup: Supine 90/90 ER position (same as PRLO start).
shoulder_hover_er.execution: Lift the back of the hand 1-3 inches off floor/block. Hold 15-30 seconds. Maintain form.
indications: Rotator cuff capacity at end range
shoulder_hover_er.progression_to: Loaded cuban press, loaded ER work
shoulder_hover_er.integration_notes: Elite-level capacity. For most IMS clients, PRLO is sufficient. Reserve hovers for athletes or clients with specific sport demands.

shoulder_loaded_car.name: Shoulder Loaded CAR (Light Band or 2-5lb)
shoulder_loaded_car.setup: Standing or tall kneeling with a light band or 2-5lb DB in the working hand. Band anchored low and in front for flexion-biased load, or behind for extension-biased load.
shoulder_loaded_car.execution: Perform a standard shoulder CAR at your typical tempo (30-45 seconds per full rotation), but now under load. The load challenges the capsule neurologically at every degree of the rotation, not just end ranges.
shoulder_loaded_car.dose: 2-3 rotations per direction per side. Full stop between reps. 45-60s rest.
indications: Owns unloaded shoulder CARs at L7-L8
Ready for capsular adaptation under load
contraindications: Shoulder CARs not yet owned at full range
shoulder_loaded_car.progression_from: Shoulder CARs L7 or L8 unloaded
shoulder_loaded_car.progression_to: Heavier loaded CARs, integrated with strength work
shoulder_loaded_car.integration_notes: Loaded CARs are the highest-level articular work. The load isn't about building 'CAR strength' - it's about neurologically loading the capsule to drive adaptation. Light loads, slow tempo.

scap_prh_depression.name: Scapular Depression PRH (Dead Hang Active)
scap_prh_depression.setup: Hanging from a pull-up bar (if bar-capable) or TRX straps (regression). Feet may touch floor with slight flex for regression.
scap_prh_depression.execution: Actively depress the scapulae (pull shoulders down away from ears). Maintain this active depressed position - not passive hanging. Chest up slightly, shoulder blades pulled down and slightly back.
contraindications: Grip strength insufficient for hang duration
scap_prh_depression.integration_notes: Scapular depression is THE gatekeeper for pull-up progressions and healthy overhead work. Most adults have lost this active control. Foundational.

scap_prh_retraction_prone.name: Scapular Retraction PRH (Prone Y/T/W Holds)
scap_prh_retraction_prone.setup: Prone on floor. Arms in Y position (overhead narrow), T position (straight out to sides), or W position (elbows bent, forearms up). Palms down or thumbs up depending on variation.
scap_prh_retraction_prone.execution: Lift arms slightly off floor into the end-range retracted position. Hold. Focus on pulling scapulae toward spine without trap shrug.
scap_prh_retraction_prone.integration_notes: Prone Y/T/W is the base of every IMS upper body day. Build capacity here before any loaded pulling gets programmed.

scap_prlo_depression.name: Scapular Depression PRLO (Active Pull-Down from Hang)
scap_prlo_depression.setup: Passive dead hang from pull-up bar, scapulae relaxed up (shrug position).
scap_prlo_depression.execution: Actively pull the shoulders DOWN into depression from the relaxed hang position. No elbow bend - arms stay straight. Hold 2-3 seconds at full depression. Relax back up. Repeat.
indications: Lat and scap depressor engagement training
scap_prlo_depression.progression_to: Scapular pull-ups, loaded pull-downs with scap focus
scap_prlo_depression.integration_notes: The 'scap pull-up' - foundational motor pattern before full pull-ups. Most adults default to biceps/arms first; this teaches scap-first mechanics.

scap_prlo_retraction.name: Scapular Retraction PRLO (Prone Active Lift-Off)
scap_prlo_retraction.setup: Prone on floor, arms in Y/T/W position at rest (arms on floor).
scap_prlo_retraction.execution: Lift arms 2-4 inches off floor actively retracting scapulae. Hold 2-3 seconds. Lower. No trap shrug - keep shoulders away from ears.
contraindications: Cannot maintain prone without cervical flare
scap_prlo_retraction.progression_to: Loaded scap retraction (light DB Y/T/W raises)
scap_prlo_retraction.integration_notes: Staple end-range scap work. Pairs with any rowing day. Upper body warm-up staple for IMS clients.

scap_err_upward_rotation.name: Scapular ERR (Upward Rotation End Range)
scap_err_upward_rotation.setup: Standing, arms overhead against wall in full flexion + upward rotation.
scap_err_upward_rotation.execution: Slow active rotation of the scapulae through the last 15-20 degrees of upward rotation - arms stay overhead, scapulae move up-down through upper ROM. Focus on serratus anterior engagement.
scap_err_upward_rotation.progression_to: Loaded serratus work (landmine press, push-up plus)
scap_err_upward_rotation.integration_notes: Under-programmed capacity. Serratus upward rotation is critical for overhead pressing and pulling health.

scap_hover_retraction.name: Scapular Retraction Hover (Sustained Y/T/W)
scap_hover_retraction.setup: Prone Y/T/W starting position with arms lifted 2-4 inches off floor.
scap_hover_retraction.execution: Hold the lifted position for 20-45 seconds actively. Scapulae remain retracted, no trap shrug, no cervical extension to cheat.
scap_hover_retraction.integration_notes: Capacity work. Most clients won't need this, but for clients with serious postural demands (cyclists, desk workers, musicians), this is gold.

scap_loaded_car.name: Scapular Loaded CAR (Light Band Resistance)
scap_loaded_car.setup: Quadruped with band looped around the working hand anchored low, or standing with light band resistance on the working side.
scap_loaded_car.execution: Perform a scapular CAR (protraction -> elevation -> retraction -> depression -> protraction in a full circle) under band resistance. Slow, controlled, 30-45 seconds per full rotation.
scap_loaded_car.progression_to: Heavier scap work integrated with upper body strength
scap_loaded_car.integration_notes: Sophisticated. Addresses capsular + scap control simultaneously. For clients doing advanced upper body work.

elbow_prh_flexion.name: Elbow Flexion PRH (Bicep Curl End Range)
elbow_prh_flexion.setup: Standing or seated, arm at side. Actively flex the elbow to fullest possible range - hand toward shoulder. No shoulder flexion to cheat.
elbow_prh_flexion.execution: Hold the fully flexed position. Biceps engaged, wrist neutral. If range is restricted, hold at wherever end range is currently.
indications: Elbow flexion ROM restriction (common post-fracture, post-immobilization)
contraindications: Acute elbow pain at end range
elbow_prh_flexion.progression_to: elbow_prlo_flexion OR loaded bicep curl work
elbow_prh_flexion.integration_notes: Often prescribed after elbow injuries or immobilization. Not a general training staple but essential for return-to-training.

elbow_prh_extension.name: Elbow Extension PRH (Full Lock-Out Hold)
elbow_prh_extension.setup: Standing with arms overhead (best shape), or prone in push-up top position with elbows locked, or quadruped with arms straight. Elbows fully extended.
elbow_prh_extension.execution: Hold the end-range extended position. Triceps engaged to maintain lock-out.
indications: Elbow extension deficit (often loss of terminal extension)
contraindications: Hyperextended elbows (don't load beyond anatomical end range)
elbow_prh_extension.integration_notes: Overhead athletes sometimes lose last few degrees of elbow extension - this rebuilds it. Also rehab-focused.

elbow_prlo_flexion.name: Elbow Flexion PRLO (Lift-Off at End Range)
elbow_prlo_flexion.setup: Lying on back with arm at side, elbow flexed to end range with hand resting near shoulder. OR seated with arm supported. Small weight (1-3 lb) held in hand optional.
elbow_prlo_flexion.execution: From the flexed position, briefly lift the forearm 1-2 inches further into deeper flexion or actively lock the position. Hold 2-3 seconds.
indications: Converting passive to active elbow flexion range

elbow_prlo_extension.name: Elbow Extension PRLO (Locked Position Lift)
elbow_prlo_extension.setup: Lying supine with arm overhead, elbow locked. OR quadruped with arm straight.
elbow_prlo_extension.execution: From locked position, briefly lift or press slightly deeper into extension. Hold 2-3 seconds.

elbow_err_flexion.name: Elbow Flexion ERR (Small-Range End Slow Curls)
elbow_err_flexion.setup: Arm at side, elbow flexed to approximately 120-140 degrees (upper end of flexion range). Light weight (0-5 lb) optional.
elbow_err_flexion.execution: Slow active rotation through the upper 20-30 degrees of elbow flexion - from fully flexed to just short of full, and back. Slow tempo (5-8 seconds each direction).
indications: Building active control at end range flexion
Biceps strength specifically at end range
elbow_err_flexion.integration_notes: Rarely programmed in isolation - usually for specific rehab or elite athletic needs.

elbow_loaded_car.name: Elbow Loaded CAR (Light Weight)
elbow_loaded_car.setup: Standing with 2-5 lb weight in working hand, arm at side.
elbow_loaded_car.execution: Perform full elbow CAR (flexion + pronation + extension + supination in combined rotation) slowly under light load. 30-45 seconds per rotation.
elbow_loaded_car.integration_notes: Niche. Usually post-rehab or advanced capacity work.

wrist_prh_extension_floor.name: Wrist Extension PRH (Reverse Prayer / Floor Palm-Down)
wrist_prh_extension_floor.setup: Quadruped with palms flat on floor, fingers pointing forward or slightly out. OR seated with palms flat on floor beside hips, fingers pointing forward.
wrist_prh_extension_floor.execution: Load weight through the hands while keeping palms flat and fingers engaged. Wrist is at full extension under body weight.
indications: Preparation for any pressing or weight-bearing on hands
Desk workers with limited wrist mobility
wrist_prh_extension_floor.integration_notes: Gateway to all pressing patterns. Bench, push-up, overhead press - all require wrist extension. Program this for clients with restricted wrists before any heavy pressing.

wrist_prh_flexion_reverse.name: Wrist Flexion PRH (Backs of Hands on Floor)
wrist_prh_flexion_reverse.setup: Quadruped with backs of hands on floor (palms up), fingers pointing toward you or out. OR seated with backs of hands on floor beside hips.
wrist_prh_flexion_reverse.execution: Load weight through the wrists in flexion. Keep arms straight or slightly bent. Hold at tolerable load.
wrist_prh_flexion_reverse.integration_notes: Less commonly programmed but valuable for clients with forearm extensor tightness.

wrist_prh_radial_deviation.name: Wrist Radial Deviation PRH
wrist_prh_radial_deviation.setup: Seated with forearm resting on thigh or table, palm down or neutral. Hand rotates to full radial deviation end range (hand deviates toward thumb side).
wrist_prh_radial_deviation.execution: Hold the end range position actively.

wrist_prh_ulnar_deviation.name: Wrist Ulnar Deviation PRH
wrist_prh_ulnar_deviation.setup: Seated with forearm resting, palm down or neutral. Hand rotates to full ulnar deviation (pinky side).
wrist_prh_ulnar_deviation.integration_notes: Paired with radial deviation for full lateral wrist capacity.

wrist_prlo_extension.name: Wrist Extension PRLO (Active Pull-Off)
wrist_prlo_extension.setup: Same as wrist_prh_extension_floor - palms flat on floor.
wrist_prlo_extension.execution: Actively try to lift the fingers or the heel of the hand off floor 1/2 to 1 inch (not much range available). Hold 2-3 seconds. Focus on active extensor engagement.

wrist_prlo_flexion.name: Wrist Flexion PRLO (Active Pull-Off)
wrist_prlo_flexion.setup: Same as wrist_prh_flexion_reverse - backs of hands on floor.
wrist_prlo_flexion.execution: Actively lift fingers or knuckles off floor 1/2 to 1 inch. Hold 2-3 seconds. Wrist flexor engagement.
wrist_prlo_flexion.integration_notes: Rare but valuable for specific grip-intensive activities.

wrist_err_flexion_extension.name: Wrist ERR (Slow End-Range Flex/Extend)
wrist_err_flexion_extension.setup: Forearm supported, hand in full extension end range (or flexion).
wrist_err_flexion_extension.execution: Slow active movement through the last 15-20 degrees of the chosen direction. Don't come out of the end range zone. 5-8 seconds per direction.
wrist_err_flexion_extension.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow reps per direction. 45s rest.
indications: Building active wrist ROM at ends
contraindications: Any wrist pain during small-range work
wrist_err_flexion_extension.integration_notes: Specialized work. Not a general population staple.

wrist_err_deviation.name: Wrist ERR (Radial/Ulnar End-Range)
wrist_err_deviation.setup: Forearm supported, hand at full radial or ulnar deviation.
wrist_err_deviation.execution: Slow active movement within the last 10-15 degrees of deviation range.
wrist_err_deviation.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow reps per direction. 45s rest.

wrist_hover_extension.name: Wrist Extension Hover (Sustained Lift)
wrist_hover_extension.setup: Quadruped with palms flat, wrists loaded in extension.
wrist_hover_extension.execution: From the loaded position, actively lift the palms 1-2 inches off floor (supported by fingertips). Hold 15-30 seconds. Active extensor engagement throughout.
wrist_hover_extension.integration_notes: Elite-level. Most IMS clients never need this.

wrist_loaded_car.name: Wrist Loaded CAR (Light Weight)
wrist_loaded_car.setup: Forearm stabilized (resting on thigh, table, or locked against torso). Light 1-3 lb weight held in hand.
wrist_loaded_car.execution: Perform full wrist CAR (extension + radial deviation + flexion + ulnar deviation in smooth rotation). Slow - 30 seconds per rotation.
wrist_loaded_car.dose: 2-3 rotations each direction per side. 45-60s rest.
wrist_loaded_car.integration_notes: Great for post-wrist-injury clients or those with long-term desk posture impacts on wrists.

hip_prh_er_90_90_front.name: Hip ER PRH (90/90 Front Leg)
hip_prh_er_90_90_front.setup: Seated in 90/90 position. Front leg is in external rotation + flexion - shin approximately parallel to the front of the mat, knee at 90.
hip_prh_er_90_90_front.execution: Hold the position. Focus on pelvis staying upright and square to the front shin. The front hip is in end-range ER-in-flexion; simply owning this shape is the work.
indications: Preparation for deeper hip work (pigeon progression)
contraindications: Acute knee pain (front knee loads in ER)
Cannot maintain pelvis upright - regress to elevated 90/90
hip_prh_er_90_90_front.integration_notes: Foundation of end-range hip ER work. If 90/90 PRH can't be held for 30 seconds, every more advanced position will fail. This is the gate.

hip_prh_ir_90_90_back.name: Hip IR PRH (90/90 Back Leg)
hip_prh_ir_90_90_back.setup: Seated in 90/90 position. Back leg is in internal rotation + flexion - thigh out to the side, shin back behind, knee at 90.
hip_prh_ir_90_90_back.execution: Hold the position. Back leg's thigh stays heavy on floor if possible (or supported if restricted).
contraindications: Cannot tolerate the back hip IR position at all
hip_prh_ir_90_90_back.integration_notes: Most adults have lost hip IR - this is the go-to position for rebuilding it. Programmed heavily with desk-workers.

hip_prh_extension_couch.name: Hip Extension PRH (Couch Stretch)
hip_prh_extension_couch.setup: Couch stretch position - back knee on floor, back foot up on wall/couch, front leg in lunge.
hip_prh_extension_couch.execution: Hold the tall-torso couch stretch position. Back glute ON. Pelvis tucked (not anteriorly tilted). This is the most intense hip flexor / anterior capsule PRH available.
indications: Hip flexor chronic tightness in desk-bound clients
hip_prh_extension_couch.progression_to: hip_prlo_extension - add active glute drive + pelvic tuck
hip_prh_extension_couch.integration_notes: Central to hip flexor work. If you program one end-range for hip extension, it's this one.

hip_prh_flexion_bear_sit.name: Hip Flexion PRH (Deep Squat or Bear Sit Hold)
hip_prh_flexion_bear_sit.setup: Deep squat hold (heels down, hips below knees) OR bear sit.
hip_prh_flexion_bear_sit.execution: Hold the deep hip flexion position. In deep squat, chest stays up. In bear sit, forearms between thighs or on floor.
contraindications: Cannot descend without heel lift (use heel-elevated regression)
hip_prh_flexion_bear_sit.progression_to: hip_prlo_flexion OR loaded deep squat work
hip_prh_flexion_bear_sit.integration_notes: Squat pattern gatekeeper. Combined with hip CARs and ankle DF work.

hip_prh_abduction_straddle.name: Hip Abduction PRH (Straddle Hold)
hip_prh_abduction_straddle.setup: Seated straddle position, legs wide to sides, knees straight, feet flexed.
hip_prh_abduction_straddle.execution: Hold the straddle position actively. Pelvis stays upright - resist collapsing backward onto sacrum. Legs pressed actively into floor.
hip_prh_abduction_straddle.integration_notes: Adductor capacity foundation. Most adults can't hold this for 30s without collapsing.

hip_prlo_er_lift_off.name: Hip ER PRLO (90/90 Front Shin Lift-Off)
hip_prlo_er_lift_off.setup: Seated in 90/90 position. Start with front shin resting on floor (PRH position).
hip_prlo_er_lift_off.execution: Briefly lift the front shin 1-2 inches off floor using active hip ER. Don't hike pelvis or lean back. Hold 2-3 seconds, return. This is active ER at end range.
hip_prlo_er_lift_off.dose: 3 x 5-8 lift-offs per side. 2-3 second hold. 60s rest.
indications: Converting passive hip ER to active
External rotator strength at end range
hip_prlo_er_lift_off.integration_notes: This is the critical step most hip programs skip. Gets active range to catch up to passive range.

hip_prlo_ir_lift_off.name: Hip IR PRLO (90/90 Back Thigh Lift)
hip_prlo_ir_lift_off.setup: Seated 90/90. Back thigh resting on floor (PRH position).
hip_prlo_ir_lift_off.execution: Briefly lift the back thigh 1-2 inches off floor using active hip IR. Extremely challenging - most clients can barely lift at all initially. Hold 2-3 seconds.
hip_prlo_ir_lift_off.dose: 3 x 5-8 per side. 2-3 second hold. 60s rest.
hip_prlo_ir_lift_off.integration_notes: One of the hardest end-range exercises in the library. Expect very small lifts initially (millimeters). Progress slowly.

hip_prlo_extension_couch.name: Hip Extension PRLO (Couch Stretch Active Glute Drive)
hip_prlo_extension_couch.execution: From the held position, actively squeeze the back glute to drive the pelvis into deeper posterior tilt - deepening hip extension. Hold the peak contraction 3-5 seconds.
hip_prlo_extension_couch.progression_to: Tall kneeling active hip extension work
hip_prlo_extension_couch.integration_notes: Glute on, hip extends. This is where clients feel the couch stretch 'work' actively.

hip_prlo_flexion_hang.name: Hip Flexion PRLO (Hanging Knee Raise to End Range)
hip_prlo_flexion_hang.setup: Hanging from a bar (active dead hang position). OR supine with hands supporting under knee.
hip_prlo_flexion_hang.execution: Actively lift knee to chest to end range of hip flexion. Hold 2-3 seconds at top. Controlled descent. In supine version, briefly lift knee out of supporting hands.
hip_prlo_flexion_hang.dose: 3 x 6-10 reps per side (supine) or bilateral (hanging). 45-60s rest.
indications: Building active hip flexion at end range
hip_prlo_flexion_hang.progression_to: L-sit progressions, hanging knee raises under load
hip_prlo_flexion_hang.integration_notes: Dual-purpose - hip flexion end range + core anti-extension.

hip_prlo_abduction_side_lying.name: Hip Abduction PRLO (Side-Lying End-Range Lift)
hip_prlo_abduction_side_lying.setup: Side-lying with bottom arm under head, top leg straight. Top leg lifted to end range of hip abduction.
hip_prlo_abduction_side_lying.execution: From maximum abducted position (not just parallel - end range), brief further lift-off attempt 1-2 inches. Hold 2-3 seconds. Focus on pure hip abduction, no pelvic hike.
hip_prlo_abduction_side_lying.integration_notes: Paired with clamshell progressions and any frontal plane lower body work.

hip_err_er.name: Hip ERR (External Rotation Slow End-Range)
hip_err_er.setup: Seated 90/90 (front leg) OR supine with hip at 90 flexion, knee at 90. Hip at end range of ER.
hip_err_er.execution: Slow active rotation within the last 10-15 degrees of ER. Don't come out of end range. 5-8 seconds per direction.
hip_err_er.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow reps per side. 60-90s rest.
indications: Building active control across end-range ER zone
hip_err_er.progression_to: hip_hover_er OR loaded hip ER work
hip_err_er.integration_notes: Refined capacity work. Transfers to squat bottom position, pigeon depth, Cossack control.

hip_err_ir.name: Hip ERR (Internal Rotation Slow End-Range)
hip_err_ir.setup: Seated 90/90 (back leg) OR quadruped with hip at 90 flexion.
hip_err_ir.execution: Slow active rotation within last 10-15 degrees of IR. 5-8 seconds per direction.
hip_err_ir.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow reps per side. 60-90s rest.
hip_err_ir.integration_notes: Rare to program - most clients never reach this level. Reserved for advanced capacity blocks.

hip_hover_er.name: Hip ER Hover (Sustained Lift-Off)
hip_hover_er.execution: Lift front shin 1-2 inches off floor and HOLD for 15-30 seconds. Maintain pelvis upright, no lean. Active ER engagement throughout.
hip_hover_er.integration_notes: Elite-level capacity. Most clients won't need this.

hip_hover_ir.name: Hip IR Hover (Sustained Back Thigh Lift)
hip_hover_ir.execution: Lift back thigh off floor and hold 15-30 seconds. Extremely challenging - may only be able to hold at very small lift initially.
indications: Owns hip IR PRLO at 8+ reps
hip_hover_ir.integration_notes: Rarely used. Most advanced hip work at IMS.

hip_hover_abduction.name: Hip Abduction Hover (Side-Lying Sustained Lift)
hip_hover_abduction.setup: Side-lying, top leg extended straight at end range of abduction.
hip_hover_abduction.execution: Hold the top leg at end range for 20-45 seconds. Active glute medius engagement. Pelvis stays stacked.
hip_hover_abduction.progression_to: Loaded side-lying work (light DB or band)
hip_hover_abduction.integration_notes: Great for runners, single-leg sport athletes, clients with hip asymmetry.

hip_loaded_car.name: Hip Loaded CAR (Ankle Weight or Band)
hip_loaded_car.setup: Standing (holding post for balance), quadruped, or side-lying. Ankle weight (2.5-5 lb) OR band resistance on working leg.
hip_loaded_car.execution: Perform standard hip CAR under load. Slow, full-range rotation through flexion + abduction + extension + IR/ER. 30-45 seconds per full rotation.
hip_loaded_car.dose: 2-3 rotations each direction per side. 60-90s rest.
contraindications: Any hip pain during unloaded version
hip_loaded_car.progression_to: Integrated loaded hip work with strength
hip_loaded_car.integration_notes: Highest level hip articular training. Transfers to all lower body strength work.

hip_loaded_car_banded.name: Hip Loaded CAR (Band Multi-Directional)
hip_loaded_car_banded.setup: Standing, band looped around working ankle, anchor point varies by direction emphasized (low for flexion-biased, side for abduction-biased, etc.).
hip_loaded_car_banded.execution: Full hip CAR under band tension. Direction of band changes capsular emphasis. Slow, controlled.
hip_loaded_car_banded.dose: 2-3 rotations each direction per side, per band angle. 60-90s rest.

knee_prh_flexion_deep.name: Knee Flexion PRH (Deep Squat or Heel-Sit)
knee_prh_flexion_deep.setup: Deep squat hold OR seated on heels (heel sit with toes pointed back).
knee_prh_flexion_deep.execution: Hold end-range knee flexion position. In deep squat, ankle DF and hip flexion also loaded. In heel sit, pure knee flexion with some quad lengthening.
contraindications: Acute knee pain in deep flexion
knee_prh_flexion_deep.progression_to: knee_prlo_flexion OR loaded deep squat work
knee_prh_flexion_deep.integration_notes: Knee flexion PRH is the bottom of the squat. If not owned, squat depth is compromised.

knee_prh_extension_full.name: Knee Extension PRH (Terminal Extension Hold)
knee_prh_extension_full.setup: Long sit OR supine with legs extended straight. Actively lock knees into full extension.
knee_prh_extension_full.execution: Quadriceps engaged, patella pulled superiorly. Hold the fully extended position - going for active terminal extension, not just passive straight leg.
knee_prh_extension_full.integration_notes: Rehab staple. Post-surgical knee clients often lack terminal extension - this rebuilds it.

knee_prh_flexion_prone.name: Knee Flexion PRH (Prone Heel-to-Glute)
knee_prh_flexion_prone.setup: Prone on floor. Bend working knee, bring heel toward glute. Can use hand or strap to pull heel closer.
knee_prh_flexion_prone.execution: Hold the end range of knee flexion. Quad lengthens.
indications: Knee flexion restriction (variant without ankle DF demand)
knee_prh_flexion_prone.integration_notes: Pairs with couch stretch work. Quad + hip flexor end range.

knee_prlo_flexion.name: Knee Flexion PRLO (Active Heel Drive)
knee_prlo_flexion.setup: Prone position with heel near glute (PRH setup) OR deep squat.
knee_prlo_flexion.execution: From end-range knee flexion, actively contract hamstrings to pull heel deeper toward glute. Hold 3-5 seconds at peak contraction.
indications: Active hamstring strength at end range knee flexion
Building active vs passive knee flexion range
knee_prlo_flexion.integration_notes: Hamstring activation at end-range - often overlooked and highly valuable.

knee_prlo_extension.name: Knee Extension PRLO (Terminal Lift)
knee_prlo_extension.setup: Long sit or supine, legs extended.
knee_prlo_extension.execution: Lift working heel off floor 1-2 inches using active terminal extension (VMO and quad lock). Hold 3-5 seconds. Return. Very small range - all about activation.
knee_prlo_extension.progression_to: Terminal extension strength work (TKE with band)
knee_prlo_extension.integration_notes: Core rehab exercise post-knee surgery. Also valuable for runners with patellofemoral issues.

knee_err_flexion.name: Knee Flexion ERR (Small Range End-Flexion)
knee_err_flexion.setup: Prone with heel at end range near glute.
knee_err_flexion.execution: Small active movements (5-10 degrees) at the top end of knee flexion. Don't drop out of end range. 5-8 seconds per direction.
knee_err_flexion.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow reps per side. 45s rest.
indications: Active range building at end-range flexion
knee_err_flexion.integration_notes: Niche. Rehab or specific sport demands.

knee_err_extension.name: Knee Extension ERR (Terminal Extension Slow Movement)
knee_err_extension.setup: Long sit, heel lifted 1-2 inches (knee extension peak).
knee_err_extension.execution: Slow active movement in the top 5-10 degrees of extension range. Heel stays lifted throughout.

knee_hover_extension.name: Knee Extension Hover (Sustained Terminal Lift)
knee_hover_extension.setup: Long sit with heel lifted at terminal extension.
knee_hover_extension.execution: Hold the lifted heel position 15-30 seconds. Active VMO engagement.
knee_hover_extension.integration_notes: Capacity work for rehab or patellofemoral-focused clients.

knee_loaded_car.name: Knee Loaded CAR (Ankle Weight)
knee_loaded_car.setup: Seated on edge of bench (legs hanging) or standing with hand support. Ankle weight on working leg.
knee_loaded_car.execution: Knee CAR - flexion to extension (and tibial rotation at each end if possible). Slow, controlled, 30-45 seconds per rotation.
knee_loaded_car.dose: 2-3 rotations each direction per side. 45-60s rest.

ankle_prh_df_kneeling.name: Ankle DF PRH (Kneeling Lunge with Loaded Ankle)
ankle_prh_df_kneeling.setup: Half-kneeling position. Lean torso forward over front foot until ankle is at end range DF. Heel stays down.
ankle_prh_df_kneeling.execution: Hold end-range ankle DF. Knee tracks over or slightly past toes. No heel lift.
indications: Ankle DF restriction - the most common lower body restriction in adults
Preparation for squat depth, split squat, lunge patterns
ankle_prh_df_kneeling.progression_to: ankle_prlo_df OR loaded calf raise work
ankle_prh_df_kneeling.integration_notes: Central to squat pattern work. Most adults have insufficient DF - this is the gateway exercise.

ankle_prh_pf_kneeling.name: Ankle PF PRH (Toes Pointed Heel Sit)
ankle_prh_pf_kneeling.setup: Kneeling on tops of feet (toes pointed away), sitting on heels. Ankles in full plantarflexion.
ankle_prh_pf_kneeling.execution: Hold the position. Weight loads the ankles in end-range PF.
ankle_prh_pf_kneeling.integration_notes: Paired with kneeling work broadly. Also addresses foot arch capacity.

ankle_prh_inversion.name: Ankle Inversion PRH (End Range Inverted Hold)
ankle_prh_inversion.setup: Seated or supine with working foot free. Actively invert (roll foot to supination - outside of foot lifted, big toe side pressed down).
ankle_prh_inversion.execution: Hold the inverted end range position.
contraindications: Recent lateral ankle sprain within 6 weeks
ankle_prh_inversion.integration_notes: Post-sprain rehab essential. Many clients have chronic ankle instability from past sprains.

ankle_prh_eversion.name: Ankle Eversion PRH (End Range Everted Hold)
ankle_prh_eversion.setup: Seated or supine with foot free. Evert foot - roll to pronation, pinky side lifted.
ankle_prh_eversion.integration_notes: Less common - usually for full ankle rehab.

ankle_prlo_df.name: Ankle DF PRLO (Heel-Loaded Lift)
ankle_prlo_df.setup: Half-kneeling with front ankle at end range DF (same as PRH).
ankle_prlo_df.execution: From the DF position, actively try to lift toes or pull knee further forward to deepen DF slightly. Hold 2-3 seconds. Return. Small range available.
ankle_prlo_df.integration_notes: Critical active-range work for any client with DF deficit.

ankle_prlo_pf.name: Ankle PF PRLO (Active Push to End)
ankle_prlo_pf.setup: Seated or kneeling in PF position.
ankle_prlo_pf.execution: Actively push toes away to deepen PF. Hold 2-3 seconds. Return.
ankle_prlo_pf.integration_notes: Pairs with calf raise strength progressions.

ankle_prlo_inversion.name: Ankle Inversion PRLO (Active Tilt)
ankle_prlo_inversion.setup: Seated or supine with foot in inverted PRH position.
ankle_prlo_inversion.execution: Actively contract to deepen inversion briefly. Hold 2-3 seconds.

ankle_err_df.name: Ankle DF ERR (Slow End-Range Movement)
ankle_err_df.setup: Half-kneeling with front ankle at end range DF.
ankle_err_df.execution: Small slow movements in last 5-10 degrees of DF. 5-8 seconds per direction.
ankle_err_df.integration_notes: Niche - for clients needing refined ankle control (running, jumping sports).

ankle_hover_df.name: Ankle DF Hover (Sustained End-Range)
ankle_hover_df.setup: Standing with one foot forward on a slight elevation, knee driven forward over toes. OR half-kneeling with deeper forward lean.
ankle_hover_df.execution: Hold the end-range DF position actively for 30-60 seconds. Calf and ankle loaded throughout.
ankle_hover_df.progression_to: Loaded ankle DF in strength work
ankle_hover_df.integration_notes: Valuable for athletes and advanced clients.

ankle_loaded_car.name: Ankle Loaded CAR (Band Resistance)
ankle_loaded_car.setup: Seated with ankle free, band looped around foot anchored to post, OR supine with band similar setup.
ankle_loaded_car.execution: Full ankle CAR (DF + inversion + PF + eversion in smooth rotation) against band resistance. Slow - 30 seconds per rotation.
ankle_loaded_car.dose: 2-3 rotations each direction per side. 45-60s rest.
ankle_loaded_car.integration_notes: Excellent for clients with chronic ankle issues.

foot_prh_toe_extension.name: Toe Extension PRH (Downward Dog or Toe-Pulled)
foot_prh_toe_extension.setup: Quadruped or downward dog with toes tucked under (toes dorsiflexed against floor). Or seated with foot manually pulled into toe extension.
foot_prh_toe_extension.execution: Hold the toe extension position. Plantar fascia is stretched.
indications: Toe extension restriction (common in dress-shoe wearers)
Big toe extension for gait mechanics
foot_prh_toe_extension.integration_notes: Hugely valuable for adults. Most have lost toe extension - this rebuilds it.

foot_prh_toe_flexion.name: Toe Flexion PRH (Curled Toes Hold)
foot_prh_toe_flexion.setup: Seated with feet free OR standing. Curl all toes under (toe flexion).
foot_prh_toe_flexion.execution: Hold the curled position. Toe flexors engaged.
foot_prh_toe_flexion.integration_notes: Underprogrammed. Foot intrinsics matter for balance and gait.

foot_prh_arch_short.name: Foot Arch PRH (Short Foot Hold)
foot_prh_arch_short.setup: Standing or seated barefoot. Actively shorten the foot by drawing the ball of foot toward heel - creating the arch without curling toes.
foot_prh_arch_short.execution: Hold the short-foot position. Arch lifted actively.
foot_prh_arch_short.integration_notes: Foundational foot work. Most adults need this.

foot_prlo_toe_extension.name: Toe Extension PRLO (Active Big Toe Lift)
foot_prlo_toe_extension.setup: Seated or standing with feet flat on floor.
foot_prlo_toe_extension.execution: Keep small toes down, lift big toe actively. OR keep big toe down, lift small toes. Isolated toe control. Hold 3-5 seconds per lift.
foot_prlo_toe_extension.dose: 3 x 6-10 per variation per foot. 30-45s rest.
foot_prlo_toe_extension.integration_notes: Surprisingly difficult for most adults. Valuable for gait mechanics.

foot_prlo_toe_flexion.name: Toe Flexion PRLO (Active Curl)
foot_prlo_toe_flexion.setup: Seated or standing on towel or rug.
foot_prlo_toe_flexion.execution: Actively curl toes to grip the towel, scrunching it toward you. Toe flexors contracting at end range of flexion.
foot_prlo_toe_flexion.progression_to: Loaded toe work (towel scrunches with load)
foot_prlo_toe_flexion.integration_notes: Great for runners and balance-impaired adults.

foot_loaded_car.name: Foot Loaded CAR (Band Resistance on Big Toe)
foot_loaded_car.setup: Seated with band around big toe, anchored under or to side.
foot_loaded_car.execution: Slow big toe CAR through all available ranges against band. 20-30 seconds per rotation.
foot_loaded_car.dose: 2-3 rotations each direction per foot. 45-60s rest.
foot_loaded_car.integration_notes: Niche but valuable for runners and clients with chronic foot issues.

cervical_prh_flexion.name: Cervical Flexion PRH (Supine Chin Tuck)
cervical_prh_flexion.setup: Supine, back of head resting on floor. Chin tucked toward chest without lifting head off floor - deep cervical flexors engaged.
cervical_prh_flexion.execution: Hold the chin-tucked position. Back of head stays grounded. Feels like pressing the back of the neck flat to the floor.
cervical_prh_flexion.integration_notes: Absolute foundation for any cervical programming. Chin-tuck is the postural cornerstone.

cervical_prh_rotation.name: Cervical Rotation PRH (End-Range Rotation Hold)
cervical_prh_rotation.setup: Supine (gravity-eliminated, preferred for restricted clients) or seated tall. Rotate head to one side to end range.
cervical_prh_rotation.execution: Hold the rotated position. No shoulder shrug, no lateral flexion creeping in.
indications: Desk-bound clients with unilateral neck stiffness
cervical_prh_rotation.integration_notes: Careful with cervical work - small movements, respect sensitivity.

cervical_prh_lateral_flexion.name: Cervical Lateral Flexion PRH (Ear-to-Shoulder)
cervical_prh_lateral_flexion.setup: Seated tall OR supine. Tilt ear toward shoulder without lifting shoulder up.
cervical_prh_lateral_flexion.execution: Hold the end range lateral flexion position. Opposite shoulder stays down.
cervical_prh_lateral_flexion.integration_notes: Often the most-needed cervical end-range for desk workers.

cervical_prlo_flexion.name: Cervical Flexion PRLO (Supine Head Lift)
cervical_prlo_flexion.execution: Maintain chin tuck while actively lifting the head 1-2 inches off floor. Hold 3-5 seconds. Don't lose the chin tuck position (no 'jutting' forward).
contraindications: Loss of chin tuck during lift (means not ready)
cervical_prlo_flexion.integration_notes: Builds the stabilizer strength that holds the head in neutral all day. Desk workers benefit hugely.

cervical_err_rotation.name: Cervical Rotation ERR (End-Range Micro-Movements)
cervical_err_rotation.setup: Supine or seated, head rotated to end range.
cervical_err_rotation.execution: Small active movements (5-10 degrees) at the end of rotation range. Don't drop out of end range. Very slow - 5-8 seconds per direction.
cervical_err_rotation.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow reps per side. 45s rest.
cervical_err_rotation.integration_notes: Advanced and careful. Most clients don't need this level.

cervical_loaded_car.name: Cervical Loaded CAR (Light Resistance / Manual)
cervical_loaded_car.setup: Seated tall OR supine. Very light resistance - either a band with light tension or coach's light hand pressure. Head is the lever.
cervical_loaded_car.execution: Perform a slow cervical CAR (flexion + rotation + extension + opposite rotation in smooth circle) under light resistance. 30-45 seconds per rotation.
indications: Owns unloaded cervical CARs at L7
Athletes with neck demands (combat sports, cyclists)
contraindications: Vascular concerns (do not program without medical clearance)
cervical_loaded_car.integration_notes: Rarely programmed at IMS. Niche exercise for specific athletic demands. Extreme caution.

tspine_prh_extension_foam.name: T-Spine Extension PRH (Foam Roller)
tspine_prh_extension_foam.setup: Supine with foam roller across upper back at mid-thoracic region. Knees bent, feet flat. Hands behind head or arms crossed on chest.
tspine_prh_extension_foam.execution: Hold the thoracic extension over the roller. Let the spine extend into the roller. Don't arch the low back - ribs stay tucked down, extension happens at T-spine.
tspine_prh_extension_foam.dose: 3 x 30-45 seconds per position (can move roller up or down to different segments). 30-45s rest.
contraindications: Osteoporosis with risk of compression fracture
tspine_prh_extension_foam.progression_to: tspine_prlo_extension OR active thoracic extension work
tspine_prh_extension_foam.integration_notes: Gateway for every overhead client. Pair with wall slides and overhead pressing prep.

tspine_prh_rotation_quadruped.name: T-Spine Rotation PRH (Quadruped End-Range Hold)
tspine_prh_rotation_quadruped.setup: Quadruped position. One hand behind head. Rotate the thorax so the elbow of the hand-behind-head reaches toward the supporting arm's wrist, then reverses to open up.
tspine_prh_rotation_quadruped.execution: Hold the end-range open rotation position (elbow pointing up and out). Shoulder blade of the rotating side moves freely.
contraindications: Shoulder pain in the behind-head position
tspine_prh_rotation_quadruped.integration_notes: Standard thoracic mobility work. Programmed frequently at IMS given desk-worker demographic.

tspine_prh_rotation_side_lying.name: T-Spine Rotation PRH (Side-Lying Open Book)
tspine_prh_rotation_side_lying.setup: Side-lying with knees stacked at 90/90, bottom arm out straight (palm up). Top arm reaches across and opens to the opposite side, creating thoracic rotation.
tspine_prh_rotation_side_lying.execution: Hold the open position with top arm reaching to the floor on the opposite side. Knees stay stacked (pelvis doesn't follow the rotation).
tspine_prh_rotation_side_lying.integration_notes: Great entry-level thoracic rotation. Often a warm-up staple.

tspine_prh_flexion_child_pose.name: T-Spine Flexion PRH (Child's Pose Reach)
tspine_prh_flexion_child_pose.setup: Child's pose - sitting back on heels with arms reaching forward on floor, forehead resting or hovering near floor.
tspine_prh_flexion_child_pose.execution: Hold the deep flexion position. Arms long, spine elongated, shoulders stretched.

tspine_prlo_extension.name: T-Spine Extension PRLO (Prone Press-Up)
tspine_prlo_extension.setup: Prone on floor with hands placed as for push-up or slightly wider.
tspine_prlo_extension.execution: Press the chest up away from floor, keeping hips heavy on floor. Extension comes primarily from the thoracic spine. Hold at peak 3-5 seconds.
contraindications: Acute lumbar pain (ensure extension comes from T-spine)
tspine_prlo_extension.integration_notes: The active version of thoracic extension work. Builds strength at end range.

tspine_prlo_rotation.name: T-Spine Rotation PRLO (Quadruped Active Open)
tspine_prlo_rotation.setup: Same as tspine_prh_rotation_quadruped - hand behind head.
tspine_prlo_rotation.execution: Actively rotate from closed position to full open position, holding the open end range 3-5 seconds. The rotation is powered actively, not swung.
tspine_prlo_rotation.integration_notes: Standard active rotation work. Pairs with core/chop-lift work.

tspine_prlo_flexion_sink.name: T-Spine Flexion PRLO (Thread-the-Needle Active)
tspine_prlo_flexion_sink.setup: Quadruped. Thread one arm under body and across, placing shoulder and side of head on floor.
tspine_prlo_flexion_sink.execution: Actively press the threaded arm into the floor, deepening the thoracic flexion + rotation combination. Hold 3-5 seconds at peak. Return to quadruped.
tspine_prlo_flexion_sink.integration_notes: Combines thoracic rotation with posterior shoulder capsule work.

tspine_err_rotation.name: T-Spine Rotation ERR (Slow Small-Range End)
tspine_err_rotation.setup: Quadruped with hand behind head, rotated to end-range open position.
tspine_err_rotation.execution: Slow small movements (5-10 degrees) within the end range zone. Don't come out of end range. Very slow.
tspine_err_rotation.dose: 3 x 5-8 slow reps per side. 45s rest.
tspine_err_rotation.integration_notes: Advanced work. Not needed for most clients.

tspine_hover_rotation.name: T-Spine Rotation Hover (Sustained Open)
tspine_hover_rotation.execution: Hold the end-range open position for 20-30 seconds. Active engagement throughout.

tspine_loaded_car.name: T-Spine Loaded CAR (Light Band Resistance)
tspine_loaded_car.setup: Quadruped or tall kneeling with band held in hand or anchored to one side.
tspine_loaded_car.execution: Perform a slow thoracic CAR under band tension - flexion, rotation to one side, extension, rotation to other side, flexion. 30-45 seconds per rotation.
tspine_loaded_car.integration_notes: High-level capacity. Rare in IMS programming but available for advanced clients.

lumbar_prh_flexion_cat.name: Lumbar Flexion PRH (Cat Position Hold)
lumbar_prh_flexion_cat.setup: Quadruped position. Round the lower back fully (cat position) - imagine pulling the belly up to the spine, tailbone tucked.
lumbar_prh_flexion_cat.execution: Hold the rounded lumbar position. Breath into the lower back.
lumbar_prh_flexion_cat.integration_notes: Use cautiously. Many clients shouldn't hold lumbar flexion. Assess first.

lumbar_prh_extension_sphinx.name: Lumbar Extension PRH (Sphinx Position)
lumbar_prh_extension_sphinx.setup: Prone, up on forearms (sphinx position). Elbows under shoulders. Pelvis heavy on floor.
lumbar_prh_extension_sphinx.execution: Hold the lumbar extension. Breath moves into the low back. Don't force if uncomfortable.
lumbar_prh_extension_sphinx.integration_notes: McKenzie-influenced rehab position. Assess extension vs flexion tolerance before programming.

lumbar_prh_lateral_flexion.name: Lumbar Lateral Flexion PRH (Side-Bend Hold)
lumbar_prh_lateral_flexion.setup: Standing or kneeling tall. Reach one arm overhead and lean to opposite side - lumbar side-bends.
lumbar_prh_lateral_flexion.execution: Hold the end-range lateral flexion. Reach long through the overhead arm.
lumbar_prh_lateral_flexion.integration_notes: Gentle lumbar work. Often overlooked direction.

lumbar_prlo_extension.name: Lumbar Extension PRLO (Prone Cobra Active)
lumbar_prlo_extension.setup: Prone, hands beside chest or in sphinx position.
lumbar_prlo_extension.execution: Lift chest off floor into cobra position actively - spinal extensors engaged. Hold 3-5 seconds at peak.

lumbar_prlo_flexion.name: Lumbar Flexion PRLO (Active Cat)
lumbar_prlo_flexion.execution: Actively round into cat position, maximum lumbar flexion, hold 3-5 seconds. Return to neutral (not extension).
lumbar_prlo_flexion.integration_notes: Use with caution. Many lumbar issues are flexion-intolerant.

lumbar_loaded_car.name: Lumbar Loaded CAR (Very Light Movement Integration)
lumbar_loaded_car.setup: Quadruped or standing. Light band around hips or very small load held in front. The 'load' is extremely light here - lumbar is not meant to be heavily loaded rotationally.
lumbar_loaded_car.execution: Slow, controlled lumbar movement through all directions - flexion, rotation, extension, rotation. Very slow. Respect the spine.
lumbar_loaded_car.integration_notes: Extremely niche. Most clients at IMS should not have loaded lumbar work programmed. Included for completeness only.

iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.name: 90/90 Front Hip ER Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.setup: Client seated in 90/90 position, front leg externally rotated at 90 degrees flexion (shin parallel to front of mat). This IS the stretched position of the anterior hip capsule / external rotators at end range ER.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.dose_prep: 2-3 rounds of the full ladder. 60 seconds rest between rounds.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.dose_spasm: 3-5 rounds. 90 seconds rest between rounds. Stop if spasm releases cleanly.
indications: PRE-heavy squat or lunge session (tissue prep)
Hip flexor / ER spasm or guarding (spasm relaxation)
Input 1 Capsular restriction that needs loaded approach
Transitioning client from PAIL/RAIL L2 to L3

iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.name: 90/90 Back Hip IR Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.setup: Client seated in 90/90, back leg internally rotated. Thigh rests out to the side at end range IR.
indications: Hip IR restriction (very common in desk-bound adults)
Pre-squat prep for clients with hip IR deficit
Input 1 capsular restriction on posterior hip
Following a PAIL/RAIL block - higher-demand progression

iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.name: 90/90 Front Hip ER Short Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.setup: Client seated in 90/90. Front shin lifts slightly off floor via active external rotation - this is the SHORTENED position of the ER muscles (they're actively contracting to hold the lifted position).
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.dose_spasm: Not recommended - use long iso for spasm work
indications: Client has passive ER but lacks active end-range strength
Input 4 Fast Twitch deficit at hip ER
Building active CARs from passive range
Converting PRLO work to capacity work
contraindications: Cannot lift shin at all (start with PRLO first)

iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.name: Pigeon Front Hip ER Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.setup: Client in pigeon position, front leg externally rotated and flexed. Can be upright or in forward fold. Deeper loading of the anterior capsule than 90/90.
indications: Pre-session tissue prep for clients doing heavy squatting
Hip flexor / ER spasm release (most effective position for this)
Input 1 + Input 3 combined deficit at hip

iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.name: Butterfly Adductor Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.setup: Client in butterfly position, soles of feet together, knees falling to sides at end range of adductor length.

iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_short.name: Butterfly Abduction Short Iso Ramp (Opposite Direction)
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_short.setup: Client in butterfly position. Knees AT end range of drop to floor.
indications: Active abduction at end range deficit
Pairing with butterfly long iso for full reciprocal work

iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.name: Straddle Adductor Long Iso Ramp (Straight Leg)
iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.setup: Client seated in straddle, legs extended wide, knees straight, feet flexed. More intense adductor load than butterfly due to extended knees.

iso_ramp_halfkneeling_hip_flexor_long.name: Half-Kneeling Hip Flexor Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_hip_flexor_long.setup: Half-kneeling position. Back leg in hip extension, hip flexor at lengthened position.
indications: Chronic hip flexor tightness / psoas guarding
Desk-bound clients with anterior hip restriction
Lower back pain with hip flexor contribution

iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.name: Couch Stretch Quad Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.setup: Client in couch stretch. Back knee on floor, foot up against wall/couch. Tall torso, active glute.
indications: Pre-squat session if quad is the limiter

iso_ramp_deep_squat_long.name: Deep Squat Hip Flexion Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_deep_squat_long.setup: Deep squat passive hold. Heels flat, hips below knees, torso upright.
indications: Input 3 Load Bearing at deep hip flexion

iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.name: Supine 90/90 Shoulder ER Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.setup: Supine, shoulder abducted 90 degrees, elbow 90, forearm at end range of external rotation (back of hand on floor or block).
indications: Anterior shoulder capsule restriction (common in lifters)

iso_ramp_standing_shoulder_flex_long.name: Wall Shoulder Flexion Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_standing_shoulder_flex_long.setup: Standing back to wall, arms overhead in goalpost against wall, elbows and wrists pressed to wall at end range of shoulder flexion. Ribs down, glutes on.
contraindications: Cannot touch wall with elbows without lumbar arch

iso_ramp_quadruped_shoulder_ir_long.name: Quadruped Thread-the-Needle Shoulder IR Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_quadruped_shoulder_ir_long.setup: Quadruped with one arm threaded under body, palm up, in the posterior capsule loading position.
indications: Anterior shoulder irritation secondary to posterior tightness

iso_ramp_quadruped_tspine_rotation_long.name: Quadruped T-Spine Rotation Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_quadruped_tspine_rotation_long.setup: Quadruped with one hand behind head. Rotated to open end range (elbow pointing up and out).
contraindications: Shoulder pain in the behind-head position

iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.name: Supine Foam Roller T-Spine Extension Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.setup: Supine with foam roller at mid-thoracic. Hands behind head, extended over the roller.
iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.dose_prep: 2 rounds per segment (mid, upper T-spine). 60s rest.

iso_ramp_halfkneeling_ankle_df_long.name: Half-Kneeling Ankle DF Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_ankle_df_long.setup: Half-kneeling. Front ankle at end range DF (knee over or past toes, heel down).

iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.name: Prone Sphinx Lumbar Extension Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.setup: Prone on forearms (sphinx). Lumbar extension position. Pelvis heavy on floor.

iso_ramp_long_sit_knee_extension_short.name: Long Sit Terminal Knee Extension Short Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_long_sit_knee_extension_short.setup: Long sit. Working leg fully extended with heel lifted slightly (terminal extension, shortened quad position).

iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.name: Supine Chin Tuck Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.setup: Supine, back of head on floor, chin tucked toward chest (deep cervical flexors at contracted position).

iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.name: Quadruped Wrist Extension Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.setup: Quadruped with palms flat, fingers forward. Wrist at end range of extension under bodyweight load.

iso_ramp_standing_foot_arch_short.name: Standing Short Foot Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_standing_foot_arch_short.setup: Standing barefoot. Actively shorten the foot (arch lifts, ball draws toward heel without toe curl).

hip_eng_goblet_squat_tempo.name: ENG Goblet Squat (Slow Eccentric to Full Depth)
hip_eng_goblet_squat_tempo.setup: Standing with goblet-held KB/DB at chest. Feet shoulder-width, slight toe-out. Core braced.
hip_eng_goblet_squat_tempo.execution: Descend through a 5-second eccentric phase into the deepest squat available - the client should feel the stretch of the hip capsule and adductors as they reach the bottom. Pause 2 seconds in the bottom (EQI moment), then stand fast (or normal speed) back up. The eccentric + bottom pause is the training stimulus - not the concentric.
indications: Building deep squat tissue tolerance under load
Client has hip IR/ER range but cannot hold load at depth
contraindications: Cannot hold deep squat passively for 30+ seconds
Acute knee or hip pain at depth
Form collapse at bottom position (regress to heel-elevated or partial depth)
coach_cues: Drive the knees out as you descend - don't let them collapse
Feel the stretch at the bottom before you stand up
Two full seconds at the bottom - count it out loud the first set
The eccentric is where the work happens - don't rush down
hip_eng_goblet_squat_tempo.progression_from: Deep squat PRH hold, heel-elevated BW squat
hip_eng_goblet_squat_tempo.progression_to: Loaded front squat tempo, barbell back squat tempo
hip_eng_goblet_squat_tempo.integration_notes: ENG goblet squat is the bridge exercise between hip articular work and heavy squat loading. Client who can't do this cleanly isn't ready for heavy barbell squats. Program 2-4 weeks before progressing to heavier external loading.
hip_eng_goblet_squat_tempo.tempo_dose: 3-5 seconds down · 2 seconds pause at bottom · 1-2 seconds up. 3-5 sets of 5-8 reps. 90-120s rest.

hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.name: ENG RDL (Slow Eccentric to End Range)
hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.equipment: KB/DB or barbell, 30-60% of training max
hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.setup: Standing, feet hip-width, slight bend in knees. Weight held at hip or racked position.
hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.execution: Hinge backward through a 5-second eccentric phase, reaching the deepest hip flexion available while maintaining neutral spine. The bar/KB tracks the legs. At the deepest position (where hamstrings begin to stretch at end range without spinal rounding), pause 2 seconds. Return to standing at normal tempo.
indications: Building hamstring / posterior chain length under load
Clients transitioning from bodyweight to loaded hinging
Sprint / athletic prep for posterior chain
contraindications: Cannot achieve neutral spine at any depth (work patterning first)
coach_cues: Push the hips BACK, not down
Keep the bar against the thighs the whole descent
Find the stretch in the hamstrings - don't round the back to get more range
Drive the floor away on the way up
hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.progression_from: BW hinge patterning, light kettlebell RDL
hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.progression_to: Heavy barbell RDL, single-leg RDL progressions
hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.integration_notes: This exercise fixes the 'hinge-from-the-back' pattern that plagues most adult clients. The slow eccentric forces proper hip-dominance because rushing is impossible. Essential pre-work for any deadlift progression.
hip_eng_rdl_slow_eccentric.tempo_dose: 5 seconds down · 2 second pause at end range · 1-2 seconds up. 3-4 sets of 5-8 reps. 90s rest.

hip_eng_split_squat.name: ENG Split Squat (Slow Eccentric to Stretch)
hip_eng_split_squat.equipment: DBs 10-30% BW each, or goblet 20-40% BW
hip_eng_split_squat.setup: Staggered stance, rear foot on floor (not elevated). Weights held at sides or goblet at chest.
hip_eng_split_squat.execution: Descend into split squat through a 5-second eccentric, reaching deep until the rear hip flexor is at full stretch. The rear knee hovers just above floor. Pause 2 seconds in the deep position. Return at normal tempo. This is the reverse lunge pattern without the step - the stance stays fixed.
indications: Hip flexor length deficit under load
Gateway to reverse lunge and step-up progressions
contraindications: Cannot maintain upright torso (regress to half-kneeling holds first)
coach_cues: Drop the rear knee straight down, don't step back first
Stay tall through the torso - the rear hip flexor should feel the stretch
Pause at the bottom, feel the front quad engage
Drive through the full foot - not just the toes
hip_eng_split_squat.progression_from: BW split squat, half-kneeling hip flexor stretch
hip_eng_split_squat.progression_to: Rear-foot elevated split squat ENG, Bulgarian split squat, reverse lunge
hip_eng_split_squat.integration_notes: Most clients have asymmetric hip extension capacity - this exercise reveals it immediately. Use as both diagnostic and training tool. The ENG version builds tissue tolerance for all subsequent split-stance work.
hip_eng_split_squat.tempo_dose: 5 seconds down · 2 second pause at bottom · 1-2 seconds up. 3-4 sets of 6-8 reps per side. 90s rest between sides.

hip_eqi_deep_squat_hold.name: EQI Deep Squat Hold (Loaded)
hip_eqi_deep_squat_hold.setup: Descend into deepest available squat with weight at chest. Heels flat, torso upright.
hip_eqi_deep_squat_hold.execution: Hold the bottom position for 30-90 seconds under load. This is not passive - maintain active engagement throughout the hold. Feet pressing into floor, knees driving out, chest up. The capsule and musculature are loaded at end range for sustained duration.
indications: Deep squat capacity building under load
Input 3 load-bearing at end-range hip flexion
Input 5 ST capacity at deep squat
contraindications: Cannot hold deep squat unloaded for 60s
Cramping in adductors or calves (reduce load or duration)
coach_cues: Active, not passive - stay engaged
Breathe through your nose - if you can't, reduce load
Press the floor away, drive knees out
If form breaks, come up briefly - don't grind through broken position
hip_eqi_deep_squat_hold.progression_from: Deep squat PRH (unloaded), goblet squat ENG
hip_eqi_deep_squat_hold.progression_to: Longer EQI durations (2+ minutes), heavier loaded goblet, split stance EQI
hip_eqi_deep_squat_hold.integration_notes: EQI holds are the purest capacity-building modality for end-range tissue tolerance. Program once per week maximum - this is demanding work. Pairs with loaded CAR work and deep squat movement training.
hip_eqi_deep_squat_hold.tempo_dose: 30-90 second holds. 2-3 rounds. 2-3 minutes rest between rounds (this is demanding).

hip_eqi_copenhagen_plank.name: EQI Copenhagen Plank (Adductor End-Range Hold)
hip_eqi_copenhagen_plank.setup: Side plank position with top leg's foot on bench, inside of ankle/shin contacting the bench. Bottom leg hovering or resting behind.
hip_eqi_copenhagen_plank.execution: Hold the side-plank position with top leg pressing DOWN into bench through the adductors. The adductor is under full load in its lengthened position. Bottom leg can be lifted for additional adductor demand or rested on floor for regression. Hold for prescribed duration.
indications: Input 3 adductor load tolerance at length
contraindications: Cannot hold a standard side plank
coach_cues: Drive the top ankle DOWN into the bench
Stack the shoulders and hips - no sag
Breathe steady - control the hold
Squeeze the glute on the top side
hip_eqi_copenhagen_plank.progression_from: Side plank, short-lever Copenhagen (foot on bench, top leg bent)
hip_eqi_copenhagen_plank.progression_to: Long-lever Copenhagen (ankle on bench), Copenhagen with bottom leg active lift
hip_eqi_copenhagen_plank.integration_notes: Gold-standard adductor capacity work. Research-backed for groin injury prevention in athletes. Also excellent for 40+ adults who train with adductor weakness.
hip_eqi_copenhagen_plank.tempo_dose: 20-45 second holds. 3 rounds per side. 90s rest.

hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.name: EQI Split Squat Bottom Hold
hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.equipment: Bodyweight initially, progressing to goblet 15-30% BW
hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.setup: Descend into the bottom of a split squat - rear knee just off floor, front thigh parallel. Optional goblet or pause position without weight.
hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.execution: Hold the deep split squat position for 30-60 seconds. Front quad and rear hip flexor are both loaded at their lengthened positions simultaneously. Active engagement throughout - front foot driving into floor, rear hip extending active.
contraindications: Cannot hold split squat bottom for 15s
coach_cues: Drive through the whole front foot
Rear knee stays just OFF the floor - not resting on it
Stay tall - don't lean over the front thigh
Active throughout - this isn't a rest
hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.progression_from: BW split squat ENG, split squat with no pause
hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.progression_to: Bulgarian split squat EQI, loaded Bulgarian split squat
hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.integration_notes: Essential bridge exercise between split stance patterning and loaded split squat/lunge work. The EQI hold builds the foundational capacity that protects the knee during dynamic patterns.
hip_eqi_split_squat_hold.tempo_dose: 30-60 second holds. 2-3 rounds per side. 90s rest.

hip_ks_cossack_squat.name: Kinetic Stretch Cossack Squat
hip_ks_cossack_squat.setup: Wide stance (beyond shoulder width), toes slightly outward. Weight held at chest (goblet).
hip_ks_cossack_squat.execution: Shift weight to one side, descending into a deep lateral squat - the working leg bends to deepest depth while the opposite leg extends fully (adductor stretch + loaded). Actively engage the working leg's quad/adductor AND the stretched leg's adductor throughout. Slowly transition to the other side, maintaining active engagement through the entire range. This is a continuous movement, not stop-and-go.
contraindications: Cannot Cossack bodyweight through full range
coach_cues: Active engagement both sides - the straight leg isn't passive
Feel the stretch AND the contraction on the straight-leg side
Drive through the full working foot, not the toes
Smooth transitions between sides - continuous movement
hip_ks_cossack_squat.progression_from: BW Cossack squat with support, butterfly iso ramp
hip_ks_cossack_squat.progression_to: Heavier Cossack with DB, dynamic Cossack to plyometric
hip_ks_cossack_squat.integration_notes: Most complete single exercise for hip frontal plane capacity. Trains both adductor lengthening AND strength simultaneously. Essential for athletes and 40+ clients who want to protect hip health long-term.
hip_ks_cossack_squat.tempo_dose: 2 seconds to descend · 2 seconds active bottom · 2 seconds to shift to other side. 3 sets of 5-8 reps per side. 90s rest.

hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.name: Kinetic Stretch Pigeon Flow (Loaded)
hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.equipment: Light DB 5-15lb held at chest OR bodyweight only
hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.setup: Start in pigeon position - front leg in ER/flexion, rear leg extended behind.
hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.execution: Actively move through pigeon variations under light load or bodyweight: (1) tall pigeon with arms overhead, (2) forward fold over front leg, (3) rotation to reach rear hand toward opposite foot, (4) back-leg quad stretch (grab rear foot). Each position held 3-5 seconds actively, flow continuous. Three complete flows per side.
indications: Combining end-range hip ER with rotation, extension, and flexion
coach_cues: Stay active throughout - these aren't static holds
Breathe through each transition
The weight increases demand - don't grip tight
Flow smoothly - don't jerk between positions
hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.progression_from: Static pigeon PRH, pigeon with rotation unloaded
hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.progression_to: Deeper pigeon flows, flow with heavier load
hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.integration_notes: Advanced integration work. Best as a finisher or recovery session component. Demonstrates the convergence of capsular work, end-range strength, and movement flow that defines FRS methodology.
hip_ks_pigeon_flow_loaded.tempo_dose: 3-5 seconds per position, 4 positions per flow. 3 flows per side. 60s rest between flows.

hip_lir_goblet_squat_ramp.name: Loaded Isoramp Goblet Squat (Tier-Paused Reps)
hip_lir_goblet_squat_ramp.setup: Standing with goblet KB/DB at chest.
hip_lir_goblet_squat_ramp.execution: Descend into squat with tier-paused descent: pause 3 seconds at quarter-depth, 3 seconds at parallel, 3 seconds at full depth. At each pause, actively contract through the available tier of effort (light at quarter, moderate at parallel, maximal at bottom). Stand normally. Each rep = one full tier ladder.
indications: Building neurological control through entire range
Addressing specific weak points in the squat
contraindications: Cannot perform standard ENG squat cleanly
Knee or hip pain at any depth
coach_cues: Feel each position distinctly
Active engagement at each pause - not a rest
Breathe between tiers
Focus on the weakest tier - that's where the work is
hip_lir_goblet_squat_ramp.progression_to: Loaded isoramp back squat with barbell, loaded isoramp front squat
hip_lir_goblet_squat_ramp.integration_notes: Advanced diagnostic and training tool. Reveals exactly where a client's squat pattern breaks down. Use sparingly - cognitively and metabolically demanding.
hip_lir_goblet_squat_ramp.tempo_dose: 3s pause x 3 positions during descent. 3 sets of 4-6 reps. 2-3 min rest.

knee_eng_sissy_squat_tempo.name: ENG Sissy Squat (Slow Eccentric)
knee_eng_sissy_squat_tempo.equipment: Bodyweight, optional 5-15lb weight held for counterbalance
knee_eng_sissy_squat_tempo.setup: Standing, heels elevated on small plate or in pike bridge setup. Hands reach forward for counterbalance OR light weight held.
knee_eng_sissy_squat_tempo.execution: Lower backwards through knee flexion - keep hips open (no hinge), torso leaning back as knees flex forward. Quad is loaded in its stretched end range. 5-second eccentric to deepest tolerable position, 2-second pause, return.
indications: Quad tendon health at extreme knee flexion
Athletes needing terminal knee flexion capacity
contraindications: Cannot tolerate deep knee flexion passively
coach_cues: Keep the hips OPEN - don't hinge
Lean back as knees go forward - this is the pattern
Don't force depth - work to current tolerance
Controlled return - don't collapse forward
knee_eng_sissy_squat_tempo.progression_to: Full depth sissy squat with added load
knee_eng_sissy_squat_tempo.integration_notes: Advanced exercise with strict prerequisites. Most IMS clients will never be programmed this - reserved for athletic populations and advanced clients with no knee history. When appropriate, builds tissue tolerance that transfers directly to deep squatting and athletic positions.
knee_eng_sissy_squat_tempo.tempo_dose: 5 seconds down · 2 seconds pause · 2-3 seconds up. 3 sets of 5-8 reps. 2 min rest.

knee_eqi_squat_parallel_hold.name: EQI Squat Parallel Hold (Wall Sit Variant)
knee_eqi_squat_parallel_hold.equipment: Bodyweight, progression: DB held at chest 15-25lb
knee_eqi_squat_parallel_hold.setup: Back against wall, descend until thighs are parallel to floor - knees at 90 degrees. Feet shoulder-width, flat on floor.
knee_eqi_squat_parallel_hold.execution: Hold the parallel position for 45-90 seconds. Knee is at 90 degrees with full body weight loaded. Active pressing into floor - not passive rest against wall.
coach_cues: Thighs parallel - not higher, not lower
Press feet into floor actively
Breathe - don't hold it
If you shake, that's normal - if you collapse, come out
knee_eqi_squat_parallel_hold.progression_to: Deeper wall sits, loaded wall sits, single-leg wall sits
knee_eqi_squat_parallel_hold.integration_notes: Classic tendon capacity exercise. Boring but effective. Programs well in 6-week tendon health blocks for runners and athletes with patellar tendon issues.

ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.name: ENG Single-Leg Calf Raise (Slow Eccentric to Deep Stretch)
ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.equipment: Stair or step edge, optional DB in hand
ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.setup: Single leg on edge of step, ball of foot on edge, heel hanging off. Opposite foot lifted or lightly touching for balance.
ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.execution: Raise to full plantar flexion (top position). Lower through 4-5 second eccentric to deepest dorsiflexion available - feel the calf / Achilles stretch deeply. Pause 1-2 seconds at bottom. Return up normally.
coach_cues: Get to the TOP - full plantar flexion each rep
Slow lower - don't drop
Feel the stretch at the bottom
Don't bounce out of the bottom
ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.progression_from: Bilateral calf raise, unassisted single-leg calf raise
ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.progression_to: Loaded single-leg calf raise, explosive calf raises
ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.integration_notes: The Alfredson protocol for Achilles tendinopathy is essentially this exercise with specific dose. Works as both rehab and prevention. Essential for runners.
ankle_eng_calf_raise_slow_lower.tempo_dose: 4-5 seconds down · 1-2 seconds pause · 1 second up. 3 sets of 8-12 reps per side. 60s rest.

ankle_eqi_deep_squat_ankle_hold.name: EQI Deep Squat Ankle Dorsiflexion Hold (Knee Past Toes)
ankle_eqi_deep_squat_ankle_hold.setup: Deep squat position OR half-kneeling with front ankle at maximum DF, knee driven well past toes.
ankle_eqi_deep_squat_ankle_hold.execution: Hold the position with maximum available DF for 30-60 seconds. In deep squat: entire body weight loads the ankle. In half-kneeling: front ankle is loaded, can rock forward for more DF demand.
coach_cues: Knee WAY past the toes - that's the point
Heel stays down
Breathe through it
Active engagement - drive the knee forward
ankle_eqi_deep_squat_ankle_hold.progression_from: Ankle DF PRH, half-kneeling ankle iso ramp
ankle_eqi_deep_squat_ankle_hold.progression_to: Loaded version with additional weight, combined with other EQI positions
ankle_eqi_deep_squat_ankle_hold.integration_notes: Critical pre-squat work for most clients. 60 seconds of this before squatting transforms the bottom position. Pairs excellently with ankle iso ramping protocols.
ankle_eqi_deep_squat_ankle_hold.tempo_dose: 30-60 seconds hold. 3 rounds per side (if single). 60-90s rest.

ankle_ks_ankle_rocker.name: Kinetic Stretch Ankle Rocker (Loaded Forward-Back)
ankle_ks_ankle_rocker.equipment: Bodyweight OR light weight vest / loaded ruck 10-20lb
ankle_ks_ankle_rocker.setup: Half-kneeling with front ankle at near-maximum DF. Body weight loaded over front leg.
ankle_ks_ankle_rocker.execution: Rock the front knee forward over the toes into maximum DF, then rock backward into PF (heel lifts slightly). Under load. Active engagement throughout - feel the calf lengthen in DF and shorten in PF. Continuous motion.
indications: Warming up before lower body training
contraindications: Balance issues (do in half-kneeling vs standing)
coach_cues: Drive the knee straight forward, not sideways
Heel stays down on the forward rock
Feel both the stretch and the push-off
Smooth continuous motion
